methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $700M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $539M - $10.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Neko Health is a Series C-stage biotech company that has raised $326M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 11 comparables: 3 public companies in Biotech & Health (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage peers including Colossal Biosciences, Chai Discovery, New Limit, and Eikon Therapeutics.

Two quantitative methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier to a $700M total-raised proxy, implying a valuation of $1.2B. Peer-median anchoring across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a $3.5B median, with a range spanning $539M (Eikon Therapeutics) to $10.3B (Colossal Biosciences). Public-comp P/S anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue figures, typical for private biotech firms.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a range of $1.0B to $4.0B, with a median of $2.3B. The wide dispersion reflects genuine heterogeneity in the peer set—particularly the $10.3B outlier in Colossal—and underscores uncertainty around Neko Health's stage readiness relative to its comparables. Capital efficiency, clinical progress, and time-to-next-milestone remain primary drivers of valuation variance within this range.
